title: [InstCombine] Decompose icmp over min/max intrinsics when compared with a constant
author: Nathiyaa-Sengodan
description:
Fold icmp pred min/max(X, Y), C (constant) into individual comparisons combined with and/or:

  min(X, Y) pred C --> (X pred C) and (Y pred C) when pred is >, >=
  min(X, Y) pred C --> (X pred C) or  (Y pred C) when pred is <, <=
  max(X, Y) pred C --> (X pred C) or  (Y pred C) when pred is >, >=
  max(X, Y) pred C --> (X pred C) and (Y pred C) when pred is <, <=

 This eliminates the min/max intrinsic and exposes simple constant comparisons that enable further optimization by downstream passes.
 
 The decomposition is restricted to cases where:
- The comparison operand C is a constant
- The min/max has one use
- The predicate is non-equality

Example:

Before :
%v = call i8 @llvm.smin.i8(i8 %x, i8 %y)
%cmp = icmp sgt i8 %v, 5

After this fold:
%cmp1 = icmp sgt i8 %x, 5
%cmp2 = icmp sgt i8 %y, 5
%res = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2

Fixes [#167059](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/167059)

tests.test_body:
define <2 x i1> @smin_commute_vec_poison_elts(<2 x i32> %x, <2 x i32> %other) {
  %notneg = and <2 x i32> %x, splat (i32 7)
  %positive = or <2 x i32> %notneg, splat (i32 1)
  %cmp = icmp slt <2 x i32> %other, %positive
  %sel = select <2 x i1> %cmp, <2 x i32> %other, <2 x i32> %positive
  %test = icmp sgt <2 x i32> %sel, <i32 0, i32 poison>
  ret <2 x i1> %test
}

define <2 x i1> @maybe_not_positive_vec(<2 x i32> %x, <2 x i32> %other) {
  %notneg = and <2 x i32> %x, splat (i32 7)
  %cmp = icmp slt <2 x i32> %notneg, %other
  %sel = select <2 x i1> %cmp, <2 x i32> %notneg, <2 x i32> %other
  %test = icmp sgt <2 x i32> %sel, zeroinitializer
  ret <2 x i1> %test
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i8 @llvm.smin.i8(i8, i8) #0

declare void @use(i8)

define i8 @test_smin_and_multiuse(i8 %x, i8 %y) {
  %x1 = and i8 %x, 31
  %y1 = and i8 %y, 31
  call void @use(i8 %y1)
  %res = call i8 @llvm.smin.i8(i8 %x1, i8 %y1)
  ret i8 %res
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
According to the IR diff in llvm-opt-benchmark I don't think this canonicalization enables more optimizations in the middle-end. BTW the decomposed form doesn't win across multiple targets: https://godbolt.org/z/sz1v3GTze

As for the motivating case, it makes more sense to convert `smin((sub nsw A, C), (sub nsw B, C))` into `sub nsw smin(A, B), C` in the middle-end: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/NJzMpH We already have similar transformations for add/shl in `foldIntrinsicUsingDistributiveLaws`.

comments.author: Nathiyaa-Sengodan
comments.body:
Makes sense. Thanks.

Let me try to implement the fold smin((sub nsw A, C), (sub nsw B, C)) into sub nsw smin(A, B), C.
